persona.name: Dimitris Iliou
persona.tagline: One hue, held to the light.
persona.bioMd:
Dimitris Iliou works in monochrome watercolor: a single earthy hue per piece, built from transparent washes that let the white paper carry the light. Sand, terracotta, olive, a muted aegean blue, or deep umber, never mixed, only deepened.

The mood is Olympia at low sun, its old sanctuary: warm stone, olive shade, and a stillness that resists being polished. Each image stays loose at the edges, with generous empty paper around a single quiet subject.
persona.processMd: Each piece starts by choosing one hue to match the emotional temperature of the source, then layering washes from a pale tint to a deep pool. Depth comes from stacked transparency, not from line or opaque paint. The paper grain and soft blooms are left in, not cleaned up.

prompt.master: Monochromatic watercolor in a single hue, transparent layered washes, wet-on-wet blooms and soft granulating edges, luminous white paper showing through, tonal range from pale tint to deep pooled pigment, visible cold-press paper grain, loose organic silhouettes, generous negative space, no opaque passages, no outlines
prompt.negative: opaque paint, flat color fill, hard vector edges, more than one hue, neon, high-key saturation, digital gradients, gloss, ink linework, text, watermark, busy background, photoreal detail

palette.description: Monochrome per artwork. Each piece uses exactly one hue from this set (sand, terracotta, olive, aegean blue, or shadow umber), expressed as a full range of transparent watercolor opacities (about 15% pale wash to 100% deep pool) over white paper. Never combine two hues in a single composition.

composition.subjectPlacement: center-weighted with generous bare paper and a high, calm horizon
composition.notes: One quiet subject rendered in loose watercolor with soft granulating edges. Depth from layered translucent washes, not line. Keep large areas of untouched white paper for breathing room.
